task_completion.description: InVesalius 3 application is launched and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 main window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The high-resolution volumetric DICOM series is selected for import, avoiding the Scout/Localizer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius DICOM import dialog, the series with >100 slices (specifically 108 slices) is selected or loaded, rather than a series with very few slices (Scout/Localizer).

task_completion.description: The 'Bone' preset is applied for masking the DICOM images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left-hand control panel under 'Select region of interest' or 'Mask', the preset dropdown is set to 'Bone', and the 2D slice views show a mask highlighting the skull/bone structures.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface model of the bone is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a human skull/cranium is visible in the bottom-right 3D viewer pane of the InVesalius interface.

task_completion.description: The 3D surface is exported as a binary STL file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D Surface' or 'Save As' dialog is visible with the path '/home/ga/Documents/axial_skull.stl', or a file manager/terminal window shows the 'axial_skull.stl' file successfully created in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to process the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progression of steps within the InVesalius interface (importing, masking, 3D viewing) rather than just executing terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The 3D model corresponds to the actual DICOM dataset provided.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D model in the viewer clearly resembles a human cranium/skull (matching the CT Cranium dataset), not a generic shape or an unrelated pre-existing STL file.

integrity.description: The STL file was exported via the application, not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export is done through InVesalius's export functionality. There should be no evidence of the agent simply using 'touch /home/ga/Documents/axial_skull.stl' or copying an existing file to fake the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct DICOM dataset is the official InVesalius CT Cranium sample, which contains exactly 108 slices. The VLM should verify that the series with 108 slices is imported, not a smaller scout series. The generated 3D model must visually resemble a human skull. The final output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/axial_skull.stl'.
